title: Special Order Decision
event: Reyes Co. has excess capacity. A customer offers $12 per unit for 5,000 units; normal full cost is $15 per unit, of which $9 is variable. Fixed costs will not change. Compute the incremental income and determine whether to accept.

computationPaths.narration: With idle capacity, fixed costs are irrelevant — they'll be paid either way. Only the variable $9 is a real cost of saying yes.

memorize.body: With excess capacity: accept if offer price > variable cost per unit. Incremental income = units × (price − VC).
The $15 'full cost' is the trap — it smuggles in fixed costs that don't change. At full capacity the answer flips: lost regular sales become an opportunity cost.

questions.prompt: Offer $12/unit, variable cost $9, full cost $15, excess capacity. Incremental income on 5,000 units = ?

distractors.feedback: You compared the $12 offer to the $15 FULL cost. Fixed costs don't change — only the $9 variable cost is relevant: +15,000.
That's total revenue (5,000 × 12). Incremental INCOME nets out the variable costs: 15,000.
